$doc: Fusion deliberation config for relentless-inception gates (v0.2.1).
USER COPY lives at ~/.claude/relentless-inception/fusion.config.json — edit THAT, not this shipped default (skill upgrades overwrite this file).
Secrets (OPENROUTER_API_KEY) go in ~/.claude/relentless-inception/secrets.env — see assets/secrets.env.example. Never put keys in this file.

transports: 'claude' = subagent inside Claude Code (no key needed) · 'codex' = codex CLI / codex@openai-codex plugin (ChatGPT subscription auth) · 'openrouter' = direct OpenRouter HTTP (needs OPENROUTER_API_KEY).
backends.codex_plugin / backends.openrouter turn the non-claude transports on or off; both may be enabled at once and seats mix freely.

Panel design guidance (TrustedRouter DRACO artifact): the FUSER is the lever (~18-pt swing) — keep it the strongest model; the judge barely matters (cheap is fine, default honors user preference of fable-5); diversity comes from distinct models/personas, NOT temperature.
$panel_notes: Default = 1 fable-5 + 1 gpt-5.6-sol (xhigh) + 1 opus-4.8 (xhigh); raise any count to 2 for heavier gates.
luna/terra are deliberately NOT in the default panel (weaker than sol) but remain valid codex models if listed here.
codex model aliases the skill resolves: sol→gpt-5.6-sol, luna→gpt-5.6-luna, terra→gpt-5.6-terra; any catalog slug also accepted, ANY effort minimal..xhigh (ultra = raw CLI only).
$fuser_note: claude-code-session = whatever model is selected in Claude Code right now runs the fuser as a fresh instance (must differ from the judge instance and from the reviewed artifact's author).

openrouter_direct.$doc: Applies ONLY when a seat (or the whole gate via the openrouter/fusion plugin) calls OpenRouter directly. OpenRouter fusion semantics: plugin temperature reaches the PANEL only; the judge is pinned to temp 0; plugin reasoning reaches panel+judge.
temp_warnings: Reasoning models generally do NOT take kindly to temperature overrides — leave temp unset/1.0 for: OpenAI o-series and gpt-5.x in reasoning mode (temperature is ignored or rejected with a 400), Anthropic Claude with extended thinking (requires temperature=1; setting anything else errors), DeepSeek-R1-class and Gemini thinking modes (sampling is managed internally).
Do not use temperature as a diversity mechanism — empirically it does not decorrelate panel errors; vary models/personas/context bundles instead.
If you set panel temp above 1.2 expect degraded instruction-following on most models; below 0.3 panels converge and the fusion gain collapses.
